data.title: Santos Bull Expects Solid 1Q Against Uncertain BackdropMarket Talk
data.description: Santos is expected by Macquarie to post a solid 1Q, including production of 22.2 million barrels of oil equivalent and revenue of US$1.3 billion. Its analysts have raised their production and revenue estimates roughly 3% and 7%, respectively, higher than before. They expect strong Papua New Guinea LNG output, with Angore online, and say Western Australia gas production looks better than their prior forecast, too. They reiterate an outperform rating. Still, the analysts pare their price target on the company's stock by 3.9% to A$8.60, citing "a minor lowering of inclusion factors on several growth projects that may face delays to FID's [final investment decisions] in the current environment." The "U.S. tariff crisis is driving more volatility and ultimately, lower oil prices," they add. Santos last traded at A$5.50. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Air New Zealand Bull Thinks It Can Handle Trade War TurbulenceMarket Talk
data.description: Although Air New Zealand has "more than its share" of uncertainties at the moment, the airline is "well positioned" to navigate them, Macquarie analysts tell clients in a research note. They say the airline is dealing with a challenging backdrop, including a combination of engine availability, weaker domestic demand and elevated competition on U.S. routes, while economic uncertainty from the trade war will hinder consumer confidence. But falling fuel prices will provide a small tailwind in the near term and a transformation program from the airline could offset any slowdown in demand, the Macquarie analysts say. They note that the airline's stock is priced at a low multiple, and see shares rising to 83 New Zealand cents, from about 59 NZ cents in recent trade. (mike.cherney@wsj.com; @Mike_Cherney)

data.title: St. Cousair to Acquire Kelly's Jelly in US Expansion
data.description:
St. Cousair subsidiary St. Cousair Inc. (SCI) will acquire Kelly's Jelly Inc.'s gourmet jam business to strengthen its US presence, according to a Friday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The acquisition is set to close April 22.

St. Cousair Inc. aims to leverage Kelly's Jelly's brand and products, including pepper jelly and fruit spreads, to expand its market reach.

data.title: China's Xi calls for greater cooperation with Vietnam
data.description:
Chinese President Xi Jinping called for stronger cooperation with Vietnam in industrial and supply chains and wider collaboration in emerging fields, the Chinese foreign ministry said on Monday.

Xi starts a three-nation tour of Southeast Asia this week, beginning his state visits with Vietnam from April 14 to 15.

Xi also urged strengthening coordination and cooperation through regional initiatives such as the East Asia Cooperation and the Lancang-Mekong Cooperation, the ministry said, citing an article by the Chinese leader published in Vietnam media.

He called such efforts necessary to "inject more stability and positive energy into a chaotic and intertwined world".

"There are no winners in trade wars and tariff wars, and protectionism has no way out," Xi said, but did not mention the United States.

"We must firmly safeguard the multilateral trading system, maintain the stability of the global industrial and supply chains, and maintain the international environment for open cooperation," he said.

In the article, Xi said China welcomes more high-quality imports from Vietnam and encourages more Chinese enterprises to invest and start businesses in the Southeast Asian country.

Both countries should expand cooperation in emerging fields such as 5G, artificial intelligence and green development, the article said.

data.title: Ansell Target Price Cut 10% to A$33.40/Share by Morgan Stanley>ANN.AU
Morito Discloses 4.36 Billion Yen Price Tag for Ms. ID Acquisition
data.description:
Morito paid 4.3 billion yen for the shares of Ms. ID Co., Ltd., with an additional 58 million yen in advisory and related expenses, bringing the total to 4.36 billion yen, according to a Friday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The deal, originally disclosed in November 2024 without a price, was already reflected in the company's earnings forecast for the fiscal year ending November 2025.

data.title: Indonesia Motorbike Sales Fall 7.2% in March
data.description:
Motorbike sales in Indonesia dropped 7.2% year-on-year to 541,684 units in March 2025, reversing a 4.0% rise in the previous month, amid weak purchasing power due to layoffs in some companies.

The decline was also impacted by the imposition of additional motorbike taxes, which took effect in 2025.

On a monthly basis, motorbike sales shrank by 6.8% in March, after rising 3.7% in February.

Last year, sales grew by 1.5%, reaching 6.3 million units, within the forecast range provided by the Association of Indonesian Motorcycle Industry (AISI) of 6.2 to 6.5 million units.

In 2025, AISI expects motorbike sales to reach a target range of 6.4–6.7 million units, before the government implements an additional motorbike tax.

AISI will recalculate its 2025 sales forecast due to the impact of the tax policy and to assess economic conditions.

data.title: New Zealand Card Spending Declines in March
data.description:
Electronic card transactions in New Zealand fell by 0.8% month-on-month to NZD 6.46 billion on a seasonally adjusted basis in March 2025.

Among retail categories, spending on durables dropped 2.5%, hospitality declined 1.1%, fuel was down 2.3%, and apparel fell 2.1%.

In contrast, spending on motor vehicles rose 2.1%, while consumables edged up 0.4%.

On a year-on-year basis, total electronic card spending decreased by 1.6% in March, marking the twelfth consecutive month of annual declines—signaling a sustained downturn in consumer spending.

data.title: Hoya Considers Lowering Investment Units to Expand Shareholder Base
data.description:
Hoya acknowledged the importance of lowering investment units to attract a broader investor base and revitalize the stock market, according to a Friday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The company stated that it would evaluate the potential for reducing investment units, taking into account stock price, market trends, shareholder composition, and the impact on current shareholders.

However, no specific plans, such as a stock split, have been finalized at this stage. This move follows the company's current investment unit of 500,000 yen as of March 31.

data.title: Gold Falls as Traders Assess Mixed Signals From Trump AdministrationMarket Talk
data.description: Gold falls in the early Asian session as traders assess mixed signals from the Trump administration. On one hand, U.S. Customs and Border Protection said Friday that computers, tablets, Apple watches, semiconductor equipment and other electronics were exempt from many tariffs on Chinese products and a 10% tariff on all U.S. imports. On other hand, administration officials including Commerce Secretary Lutnick said on Sunday that tech products exempted from many tariffs will face separate levies in a month or two. "Gold's downside is extremely limited," TD Securities' Daniel Ghali says in research report. The senior commodity strategist notes factors including upcoming Chinese life insurance companies' purchases possibly leading to a massive new buying impulse. Spot gold is 0.5% lower at $3,219.18/oz. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Lynas Rare Earths Target Price Raised 2.8% to A$7.30/Share by Macquarie>LYC.AU
Black Cat Strikes Visible Gold at Western Australia Project
data.description:
Black Cat Syndicate (ASX: BC8) struck visible gold mineralization at its Kal East Gold project in Western Australia, with assay results from the ongoing drilling expected later this month, according to a Monday filing with the Australian bourse.

Additionally, the company plans to start a 20,000-meter drilling program at its Fingals Gold project this month, the filing said.

Draft to Spin Off Design Unit into New Architecture Subsidiary
data.description:
Draft will establish a wholly owned subsidiary in April to advance its high-profile "leading projects," according to a Friday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The new unit, named TJ Design and Architecture, will focus on planning, design and consulting for buildings, interiors, and urban spaces. Taiju Yamashita, who has led the internal team handling such projects, will serve as representative director.

The move aligns with Draft's medium-term plan through FY26, which seeks to leverage synergies between its regular, proposal-based, and leading projects.

Operations are scheduled to begin in May.

Ebos Target Price Cut 0.7% to NZ$41.72/Share by Macquarie>EBO.NZ
Tonami Holdings to Change Parent as JWT Tender Offer Succeeds
data.description:
Tonami Holdings said the tender offer launched by JWT Co., a subsidiary of Japan Post Holdings , closed successfully on April 10 with 7.9 million shares tendered, exceeding the minimum requirement of 6 million shares, according to a Friday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

JWT is set to become Tonami's new parent and largest shareholder with an 87.24% stake once settlement begins on April 17. Through JWT, both Japan Post Co. and Japan Post Holdings will also become indirect parents of the company.

Japan Post Holdings, headquartered in Tokyo, reported capital of 3.5 trillion yen as of April 11. Its main shareholders include the Minister of Finance and domestic trust banks.

Tonami shares are currently listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange's Prime Market.

However, the company reiterated that it may be taken private under a two-step acquisition process. If completed, Tonami is expected to be delisted.

data.title: Trump-appointed official, who oversaw dismantling of USAID, leaves State Department
data.description:
Pete Marocco, the Trump administration official who played a major role in dismantling the U.S. Agency for International Development, has left the State Department, a U.S. official said on Sunday.

President Donald Trump's administration has moved to fire nearly all USAID staff, as billionaire Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency has slashed funding and dismissed contractors across the federal bureaucracy in what it calls an attack on wasteful spending.

"Pete was brought to State with a big mission — to conduct an exhaustive review of every dollar spent on foreign assistance. He conducted that historic task and exposed egregious abuses of taxpayer dollars," a senior administration official said, speaking on the condition of anonymity.

"We all expect big things are in store for Pete on his next mission," the official added.

Sources told Reuters that Marocco, who was the director of foreign assistance at the State Department, may have been pushed out but they declined to give further explanation.

As recently as Thursday, he held a "listening session" at the State Department with nearly two dozen experts to discuss the future of foreign assistance and seek input, according to a source familiar with the event and an invitation to the session seen by Reuters.

When he returned to the State Department less than a month ago, he said in an internal email that "I am going to return to my post as the Director of Foreign Assistance to bring value back to the American people."

Trump has claimed without evidence that USAID was rife with fraud and run by "radical left lunatics," while Musk falsely accused it of being a "criminal" organization.

Trump's administration on Tuesday moved to reinstate at least six recently canceled U.S. foreign aid programs for emergency food assistance.

The quick reversal of decisions made just days ago underscored the rapid-fire nature of Trump's cuts to foreign aid. That has led to programs being cut, restored then cut again, disrupting international humanitarian operations.

data.title: Santos Target Price Cut 3.9% to A$8.60/Share by Macquarie>STO.AU

Olam Group: To Finance Reorganization Plan From Two Main Sources of Funds
Kotobuki Spirits Raises Dividend Forecast to 32 Yen on Strong Performance
data.description:
Kotobuki Spirits raised its year-end dividend forecast for the fiscal year ending March 31, to 32 yen per share from the previously planned 28 yen, citing strong business performance, according to a Friday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The increase will be proposed at the 73rd ordinary general shareholders meeting scheduled for June. The company had not paid a second-quarter dividend.

For the previous fiscal year, it paid a full-year dividend of 28 yen.
